message:
fix: mirror FTS rebuild fixes in the rusqlite fallback path

The rusqlite-backed FTS functions (rebuild_fts_via_rusqlite and the
incremental catch-up inside ensure_fts_consistency_via_rusqlite) carried
the same two bugs that existed in the frankensqlite path:

1. INNER JOIN on agents silently dropped messages whose conversation had
   a NULL agent_id, diverging from the Tantivy lexical rebuild path
   (which degrades to 'unknown' per 8a0c547c) and from the frankensqlite
   FTS path after e1c08e7c.  Switched to LEFT JOIN + COALESCE(a.slug,
   'unknown') so both paths index the same rows with the same slugs.

2. The incremental catch-up triggered a full rebuild every time the
   message<->fts row count disagreed, even when the gap was caused by
   un-indexable orphaned messages (dangling conversation_id).  Mirror
   the frankensqlite fix from da00f8ad: if incremental inserted zero
   rows yet the gap remains, the gap is permanent, so short-circuit to
   IncrementalCatchUp instead of re-running a rebuild that will just
   re-exclude the same orphans on every startup.

Standard SQLite's query planner handles multi-table LEFT JOIN
efficiently via primary-key index lookups, so the rusqlite path doesn't
need the correlated-subquery formulation that the frankensqlite path
uses to avoid materialization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -853,10 +853,10 @@ pub(crate) fn rebuild_fts_via_rusqlite(db_path: &Path) -> Result<usize> {
     let inserted = tx
         .execute(
             "INSERT INTO fts_messages(rowid, content, title, agent, workspace, source_path, created_at)
-             SELECT m.id, m.content, c.title, a.slug, w.path, c.source_path, m.created_at
+             SELECT m.id, m.content, c.title, COALESCE(a.slug, 'unknown'), w.path, c.source_path, m.created_at
              FROM messages m
              JOIN conversations c ON m.conversation_id = c.id
-             JOIN agents a ON c.agent_id = a.id
+             LEFT JOIN agents a ON c.agent_id = a.id
              LEFT JOIN workspaces w ON c.workspace_id = w.id
              ORDER BY m.rowid",
             [],
@@ -952,13 +952,12 @@ pub(crate) fn ensure_fts_consistency_via_rusqlite(db_path: &Path) -> Result<FtsC
     let inserted_rows = tx
         .execute(
             "INSERT INTO fts_messages(rowid, content, title, agent, workspace, source_path, created_at)
-             SELECT m.id, m.content, c.title, a.slug, w.path, c.source_path, m.created_at
+             SELECT m.id, m.content, c.title, COALESCE(a.slug, 'unknown'), w.path, c.source_path, m.created_at
              FROM messages m
              JOIN conversations c ON m.conversation_id = c.id
-             JOIN agents a ON c.agent_id = a.id
+             LEFT JOIN agents a ON c.agent_id = a.id
              LEFT JOIN workspaces w ON c.workspace_id = w.id
-             LEFT JOIN fts_messages f ON f.rowid = m.id
-             WHERE f.rowid IS NULL
+             WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM fts_messages f WHERE f.rowid = m.id)
              ORDER BY m.rowid",
             [],
         )
@@ -980,6 +979,25 @@ pub(crate) fn ensure_fts_consistency_via_rusqlite(db_path: &Path) -> Result<FtsC
         });
     }
 
+    // Same un-indexable-gap short-circuit as the frankensqlite path: if the
+    // incremental catch-up inserted zero rows yet the gap between
+    // total_messages and indexed_messages persists, the remaining messages
+    // are orphans (dangling conversation_id) and a full rebuild would just
+    // re-exclude them.  Accept the current state instead of looping.
+    if inserted_rows == 0 {
+        tracing::debug!(
+            db_path = %db_path.display(),
+            indexed_messages = repaired_rows,
+            total_messages,
+            un_indexable_gap = total_messages.saturating_sub(repaired_rows),
+            "FTS catch-up (rusqlite) inserted 0 rows; remaining gap is un-indexable (likely orphaned messages)"
+        );
+        return Ok(FtsConsistencyRepair::IncrementalCatchUp {
+            inserted_rows: 0,
+            total_rows: usize::try_from(repaired_rows.max(0)).unwrap_or(usize::MAX),
+        });
+    }
+
     drop(conn);
     let inserted_rows = rebuild_fts_via_rusqlite(db_path)?;
     Ok(FtsConsistencyRepair::Rebuilt { inserted_rows })
